closed_loop.tooltip: Whether to close the context window loop; only applicable to looped schedules.

output_tooltips: The model with context windows applied during sampling.

ApplyRifleXRoPE_WanVideo.name: ApplyRifleXRoPE_WanVideo

rel_l1_thresh.tooltip: Threshold for to determine when to apply the cache, compromise between speed and accuracy. When using coefficients a good value range is something between 0.2-0.4 for all but 1.3B model, which should be about 10 times smaller, same as when not using coefficients.

start_percent.tooltip: The start percentage of the steps to use with TeaCache.

end_percent.tooltip: The end percentage of the steps to use with TeaCache.

coefficients.tooltip: Coefficients for rescaling the relative l1 distance, if disabled the threshold value should be about 10 times smaller than the value used with coefficients.

WanVideoTeaCacheKJ.name: WanVideoTeaCacheKJ
WanVideoTeaCacheKJ.description:
Patch WanVideo model to use TeaCache. Speeds up inference by caching the output and applying it instead of doing the step. Best results are achieved by choosing the appropriate coefficients for the model. Early steps should never be skipped, with too aggressive values this can happen and the motion suffers. Starting later can help with that too. When NOT using coefficients, the threshold value should be about 10 times smaller than the value used with coefficients. Official recommended values https://github.com/ali-vilab/TeaCache/tree/main/TeaCache4Wan2.1: 
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+
|       Model       |  Low   | Medium  |  High  |
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+
| Wan2.1 t2v 1.3B  |  0.05  |  0.07   |  0.08  |
| Wan2.1 t2v 14B   |  0.14  |  0.15   |  0.20  |
| Wan2.1 i2v 480P  |  0.13  |  0.19   |  0.26  |
| Wan2.1 i2v 720P  |  0.18  |  0.20   |  0.30  |
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+

latent.tooltip: Only used to get the latent count

SkipLayerGuidanceWanVideo.name: SkipLayerGuidanceWanVideo
SkipLayerGuidanceWanVideo.description: Simplified skip layer guidance that only skips the uncond on selected blocks

nag_alpha.tooltip: Mixing coefficient in that controls the balance between the normalized guided representation and the original positive representation.

nag_tau.tooltip: Clipping threshold that controls how much the guided attention can deviate from the positive attention.

enable_vae_tiling.tooltip: Drastically reduces memory use but will introduce seams at tile stride boundaries. The location and number of seams is dictated by the tile stride size. The visibility of seams can be controlled by increasing the tile size. Seams become less obvious at 1.5x stride and are barely noticeable at 2x stride size. Which is to say if you use a stride width of 160, the seams are barely noticeable with a tile width of 320.

tile_x.tooltip: Tile width in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will make seams more obvious.

tile_y.tooltip: Tile height in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will make seams more obvious.

tile_stride_x.tooltip: Tile stride width in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will introduce more seams.

tile_stride_y.tooltip: Tile stride height in pixels. Smaller values use less VRAM but will introduce more seams.

use_disk_cache.tooltip: Cache the text embeddings to disk for faster re-use, under the custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper/text_embed_cache directory

device.tooltip: Device to run the text encoding on.

WanVideoTextEncodeCached.name: WanVideoTextEncodeCached
WanVideoTextEncodeCached.description:
Encodes text prompts into text embeddings. This node loads and completely unloads the T5 after done,  
leaving no VRAM or RAM imprint. If prompts have been cached before T5 is not loaded at all.  
negative output is meant to be used with NAG, it contains only negative prompt embeddings.  

Additionally you can provide a Qwen LLM model to extend the positive prompt with either one  
of the original Wan templates or a custom system prompt.
output_tooltips: The text embeddings for both prompts
The text embeddings for the negative prompt only (for NAG)
Positive prompt to display prompt extender results

Scaled modes only work with matching weights, _fast modes (fp8 matmul) require CUDA compute capability >= 8.9 (NVIDIA 4000 series and up), e4m3fn generally can not be torch.compiled on compute capability < 8.9 (3000 series and under)

rms_norm_function.tooltip: RMSNorm function to use, 'pytorch' is the new native torch RMSNorm, which is faster (when not using torch.compile mostly) but changes results slightly. 'default' is the original WanRMSNorm

lora.tooltip: LORA models are expected to be in ComfyUI/models/loras with .safetensors extension

merge_loras.tooltip: Merge LoRAs into the model, otherwise they are loaded on the fly. Always disabled for GGUF and scaled fp8 models. This affects ALL LoRAs, not just the current one

rel_l1_thresh.tooltip: Higher values will make TeaCache more aggressive, faster, but may cause artifacts. Good value range for 1.3B: 0.05 - 0.08, for other models 0.15-0.30

start_step.tooltip: Start percentage of the steps to apply TeaCache

use_coefficients.tooltip: Use calculated coefficients for more accuracy. When enabled therel_l1_thresh should be about 10 times higher than without

mode.tooltip: Choice between using e (time embeds, default) or e0 (modulated time embeds)

WanVideoTeaCache.name: WanVideoTeaCache
WanVideoTeaCache.description:
Patch WanVideo model to use TeaCache. Speeds up inference by caching the output and applying it instead of doing the step. Best results are achieved by choosing the appropriate coefficients for the model. Early steps should never be skipped, with too aggressive values this can happen and the motion suffers. Starting later can help with that too. When NOT using coefficients, the threshold value should be about 10 times smaller than the value used with coefficients. Official recommended values https://github.com/ali-vilab/TeaCache/tree/main/TeaCache4Wan2.1: 
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+
|       Model       |  Low   | Medium  |  High  |
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+
| Wan2.1 t2v 1.3B  |  0.05  |  0.07   |  0.08  |
| Wan2.1 t2v 14B   |  0.14  |  0.15   |  0.20  |
| Wan2.1 i2v 480P  |  0.13  |  0.19   |  0.26  |
| Wan2.1 i2v 720P  |  0.18  |  0.20   |  0.30  |
+-------------------+--------+---------+--------+

magcache_thresh.tooltip: How strongly to cache the output of diffusion model. This value must be non-negative.

magcache_K.tooltip: The maxium skip steps of MagCache.

easycache_thresh.tooltip: How strongly to cache the output of diffusion model. This value must be non-negative.

generation_width.tooltip: Width of the image to encode

generation_height.tooltip: Height of the image to encode

noise_aug_strength.tooltip: Strength of noise augmentation, helpful for I2V where some noise can add motion and give sharper results

latent_strength.tooltip: Additional latent multiplier, helpful for I2V where lower values allow for more motion

adjust_resolution.tooltip: Performs the same resolution adjustment as in the original code

max_new_tokens.tooltip: Maximum number of new tokens to generate.

device.tooltip: Device to run the model on. Default uses the main device.

force_offload.tooltip: Force offload the model to the offload device after generation. Useful for large models.

system_prompt.tooltip: System prompt to use for the model.

custom_system_prompt.tooltip: Custom system prompt to use instead of the predefined ones.

system_prompt.tooltip: System prompt to use for the model.

custom_system_prompt.tooltip: Custom system prompt to use instead of the predefined ones.

frame_window_size.tooltip: Number of frames in a single window

enable_framepack.tooltip: Enable Framepack sampling loop, not compatible with context windows

force_offload.tooltip: Moves the model to the offload device after sampling

riflex_freq_index.tooltip: Frequency index for RIFLEX, disabled when 0, default 6. Allows for new frames to be generated after without looping

samples.tooltip: init Latents to use for video2video process

batched_cfg.tooltip: Batch cond and uncond for faster sampling, possibly faster on some hardware, uses more memory

rope_function.tooltip: Comfy's RoPE implementation doesn't use complex numbers and can thus be compiled, that should be a lot faster when using torch.compile. Chunked version has reduced peak VRAM usage when not using torch.compile

start_step.tooltip: Start step for the sampling, 0 means full sampling, otherwise samples only from this step

end_step.tooltip: End step for the sampling, -1 means full sampling, otherwise samples only until this step

add_noise_to_samples.tooltip: Add noise to the samples before sampling, needed for video2video sampling when starting from clean video
